seoTitle: How to Add Images to Data Cards in Power BI
metaDescription: Insert dynamic logos and icons into Power BI card visuals by categorizing columns as Image URL data and formatting the card's visual container.
intro: Flat text-only KPI grids can undersell important executive metrics. Power BI lets you bind dynamic web icons and logos directly into card visuals by categorizing your data columns correctly. This guide covers setting up the data category and formatting the card so images render without distortion.

steps.heading: Categorize the data column as Image URL
steps.body: Select the column containing your image links and change its data category to Image URL to prevent rendering errors.

steps.heading: Open the card formatting panel
steps.body: Access the upgraded visual formatting container for your card visual to begin adjusting image display options.

steps.heading: Adjust alignment and transparency
steps.body: Set image alignment and transparency constraints so the graphic fits cleanly within the card's layout.

steps.heading: Map conditional icons to KPI states
steps.body: Configure rules that swap icons based on KPI performance states, giving viewers an at-a-glance visual cue.

steps.heading: Package the layout responsively
steps.body: Check that the card visual maintains a clean, fluid layout across different report sizes and screen resolutions.

faq.question: Why isn't my image showing up in the card visual?
faq.answer: This is usually because the column hasn't been categorized as Image URL, which is required for Power BI to render it as a graphic instead of text.

faq.question: Can I use conditional icons based on performance?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map different icons to different KPI states so the card visually communicates status at a glance.

faq.question: What image formats work best in data cards?
faq.answer: Standard web image formats accessible via URL work best, since the visual pulls the image directly from the link.

faq.question: Can I add a background logo to a KPI card?
faq.answer: Yes, the card formatting panel supports inserting static brand logos or background graphics into the card frame.
